description: HygR (hph / APH(4)-Ia) is the E. coli hygromycin B 4-O-phosphotransferase that confers hygromycin B resistance by ATP/GTP-dependent phosphorylation of the antibiotic's cyclitol 4-OH.

references.title: Plasmid-encoded hygromycin B resistance: the sequence of hygromycin B phosphotransferase gene and its expression in Escherichia coli and Saccharomyces cerevisiae

references.authors: Stogios PJ, Shakya T, Evdokimova E, Savchenko A, Wright GD
references.title: Structure and function of APH(4)-Ia, a hygromycin B resistance enzyme

references.authors: Iino D, Takakura Y, Fukano K, Sasaki Y, Hoshino T, Ohsawa K, Nakamura A, Yajima S
references.title: Crystal structures of the ternary complex of APH(4)-Ia/Hph with hygromycin B and an ATP analog using a thermostable mutant

label: HygR
note: HygR (hph / APH(4)-Ia) is the E. coli hygromycin B 4-O-phosphotransferase that confers hygromycin B resistance by ATP/GTP-dependent phosphorylation of the antibiotic's cyclitol 4-OH.

label: N-terminal lobe
note: Beta-rich N-terminal lobe of the protein-kinase-like (APH) fold.

label: C-terminal lobe
note: Predominantly alpha-helical C-terminal lobe (residues 99-301, 1-based; Stogios et al.

label: Mg2+-binding aspartate
note: Asp216 (in the I215-D216-W217-S218 loop) is the Mg2+-coordinating aspartate (DFG second-Asp equivalent of protein kinases): it contacts Mg2+ and a non-bridgi...
